summary: Cross-platform SIGKILL/restore evidence passed: all scenarios preserved data integrity.

scenarios.evidence: Node.js process killed via SIGKILL; session JSONL recovered intact on restart.
Docker container stopped via docker stop; session JSONL recovered intact on container restart.
Simulated crash mid-write; session JSONL partial-last-line recovery preserved data integrity.

nonGoals: D136 does not expand default registry exposure; the default profile remains 21 tools (coding + Code Intel essentials).
D136 does not implement Browser, Desktop, Channel, media, productivity, or hosted defaults.
D136 does not claim v3.0, v4.0, or v1-v4 production completion beyond the SIGKILL/restore check.
